context: Tests whether the agent implements slot booking with proper concurrency control — using database-level row locking and a single atomic transaction — and correctly handles the three failure conditions (slot inactive, slot full, cutoff passed) while incrementing the booked count safely.

checklist.name: Transaction wrapping
checklist.description: The entire booking operation (read slot, validate, increment booked, create assignment) is wrapped in a single database transaction

checklist.name: SELECT FOR UPDATE
checklist.description: The slot row is fetched inside the transaction using SELECT ... FOR UPDATE (or equivalent advisory/row lock) before validation and update

checklist.name: Capacity check in transaction
checklist.description: The check booked >= capacity (or booked < capacity) is performed INSIDE the transaction, after the lock is acquired — not before the transaction begins

checklist.name: Cutoff check in transaction
checklist.description: The check that current time has not passed cutoff_time is performed INSIDE the transaction

checklist.name: is_active check
checklist.description: The slot's is_active field is checked inside the transaction and booking is rejected if the slot is not active

checklist.name: Atomic booked increment
checklist.description: The booked count is incremented with a SQL expression (e.g. booked = booked + 1) rather than reading the current value in application code and writing back a calculated number

checklist.name: Assignment record created
checklist.description: A delivery_assignments row with status 'pending' is inserted inside the same transaction as the booked increment

checklist.name: SLOT_FULL error raised
checklist.description: A clear error (exception, rejected promise, or error response) is thrown/returned when booked >= capacity

checklist.name: SLOT_CUTOFF_PASSED error raised
checklist.description: A clear error is thrown/returned when the current time is past cutoff_time

checklist.name: No pre-transaction validation
checklist.description: The implementation does NOT do a capacity or cutoff check outside the transaction (e.g. an unprotected findById before opening the transaction) as the sole guard
